channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Municipal Financial Mismanagement and Legal Cases Highlighted in Sowetan's Top Reads
item.description: Sowetan’s latest edition highlights critical issues facing South Africa. The first story focuses on Gauteng municipalities owing over R7bn to water boards and Eskom, with the National Treasury withhol...

item.title: Health ombud reveals findings into KZN healthcare worker deaths
item.description: The health ombud, Prof Taole Mokoena, is set to brief the media on the outcomes of a joint investigation into recent fatalities involving healthcare professionals in KwaZulu-Natal public health facili...

item.title: Telstra Mobile Outage Disrupts Train Services Across Australia
item.description: A major Telstra mobile network outage on 8 July 2026 caused widespread disruptions across Australia, affecting train services in Victoria and New South Wales. The incident highlighted the critical rel...

item.title: Pastor Kanyari fights custody battle over children's education plans
item.description: Pastor Victor Kanyari has entered a legal battle over custody of his two children with their stepfather, Hiram Gitau. The dispute stems from allegations that Gitau is blocking Kanyari from contacting ...

item.title: Brian Paisley, Founder of Edmonton Fringe Festival, Dies at 80
item.description: Brian Paisley, the visionary founder of the Edmonton International Fringe Theatre Festival—the longest-running Fringe event in North America—has passed away after a prolonged battle with pneumonia. He...

item.title: Call for National Law on Transitional Justice for Bangsamoro People
item.description: Peace advocates in the Philippines are pushing for a national law to implement transitional justice and reconciliation for the Bangsamoro people, emphasizing that reparations must come from the Philip...

item.title: Tanzania's Commonwealth Games Team Loses Key Athletes, Affects Medal Hopes
item.description: Tanzania will compete in the 2026 Commonwealth Games without its top distance runners, including Alphonce Simbu, Gabriel Geay, Emmanuel Giniki, and Magdalena Shauri, due to scheduling conflicts. This ...

item.title: The Rise of Surveillance and Control in America's 'Papers, Please' State
item.description: This article explores the growing surveillance state in the United States, driven by policies like interior checkpoints, facial recognition technology, and biometric tracking. It traces the evolution ...

item.title: Trump Declares Iran Ceasefire Over Amid Escalating Tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump declared the ceasefire with Iran officially over after major clashes between US forces and Iranian troops. He accused Tehran of lying about the terms of the June 17 agreement...

item.title: Social Media Influencers Shape Gen Z Financial Decisions in Tanzania
item.description: A new study by researchers from the University of Dodoma highlights how social media personalities, known as 'finfluencers,' are significantly impacting young investors' financial choices in Tanzania....

item.title: Eddie Butita Reveals Origin of DJ Shiti Character During Visit to Nikita Kering's Mansion
item.description: Comedian Eddie Butita has shared how the iconic DJ Shiti character was born during a visit to singer Nikita Kering's family home. Speaking in an interview, Butita recalled his first-ever mansion exper...

item.title: Mark Carney reshapes Senate with partisan appointments and revised guidelines
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is introducing changes to the Senate that signal a shift from Justin Trudeau's nonpartisan reforms. His appointments of Tom Pitfield, a longtime Liberal strategist, and Rich...

item.title: Egypt Coach Alleges Racism After Disallowed Goals in World Cup Clash with Argentina
item.description: Egypt's coach Hossam Hassan sparked controversy by alleging racism following a disallowed goal in their Round of 16 defeat to Argentina during the FIFA World Cup 2026. The incident occurred after refe...

item.title: North Dakota leaders discuss Trump, Roosevelt, and bison conservation
item.description: The article discusses a conversation between North Dakota leaders and historical figures, blending political commentary with cultural references. While the original title sensationalizes the topic by ...

item.title: UK Faces Prolonged Heatwave with Health Alerts and High Temperatures
item.description: The UK is experiencing one of its longest-lasting heatwaves since 1976, with amber and yellow heat-health alerts in place across England. Temperatures could reach up to 36°C (97°F) in southern regions...

item.title: President Tinubu Launches Presidential Working Group for National Policing Bill Implementation
item.description: President Bola Tinubu, represented by Chief of Staff Femi Gbajabiamila, officially launched the Presidential Working Group tasked with drafting the National Policing Bill to implement state police acr...

item.title: Day 3 of Vice President Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial Proceeds
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte entered its third day with ongoing hearings and legal proceedings. Key witnesses, including the Ombudsman and legal experts, testified about the al...

item.title: Regional unity emerges after China's South Pacific missile test
item.description: China's recent test of a nuclear-capable missile over the South Pacific has sparked significant regional diplomatic responses, with Australia's neighboring countries demonstrating a unified stance. Th...

item.title: 2026 World Cup Takes One-Day Break for Team Recovery
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup, which is being held in the US, Canada, and Mexico, has scheduled a one-day break after the knockout stage began. This pause allows teams to recover before the quarter-finals, ...

item.title: Kenya Activists Demand Parliamentary Inquiry into Security Agency Alleged Rights Abuses
item.description: Human rights campaigners in Kenya are pushing for the formation of a joint parliamentary committee to investigate alleged human rights violations by security agencies. The proposal, which was part of ...
